instructions: Cream butter, sugar, eggs, maple extract, and baking soda until light and fluffy. Stir in flour until just incorporated. Stir in chocolate chips. Bake at 350 degrees for 10 minutes
author: Jennifer Malan

instructions: Heat oil in a large pot and cook onion, carrot, and celery until onion is translucent. Add broth and seasonings and bring to a simmer. Add noodles and simmer until tender. Stir in chicken and peas
author: Jennifer Malan

instructions: Melt butter in a large pot, then stir in marshmallows and stir constantly until melted. Stir in vanilla.  Stir in cereal and lightly press into a greased 9x13 pan. Sprinkle with MnMs if desired
author: Jennifer Malan

instructions: Combine all and simmer for about 4 hours until reduced by about half. Blend with an immersion blender until smooth. Serve as desired and freeze in smaller quantities for future meals.
author: Jennifer Malan

ingredients: 2 packages ramen noodles, crushed (discard flavor packets
instructions: Brown noodles and almonds in 3T of the oil and set aside to cool. Combine chicken, cabbage, green onion in a large bowl. In a small bowl combine remaining oil, vinegar, sugar, salt, pepper, and soy sauce.  Pour over chicken and cabbage mixture and toss to cominbe. Just before serving, stir in browned noodles and almonds.
author: Jennifer Malan

instructions: Combine AP flour, yeast, sugar, milk, and oil. Add about 4 cups of bread flour until shaggy. Cover and let rise for 20 min. Add salt and 1 cup of flour until the dough is right. Rise until doubled. Shape into 3 loaves, and rise again. Bake at 375 F for about 35 min.
author: Jennifer Malan

instructions: Put the flour, seasoning salt, and pepper in one bowl, beat the eggs and put them in the second bowl, and put the panko, italian seasoning, and parmesan in the third bowl. Roll chicken in each and bake for about 20 min on 450.
author: Jennifer Malan

instructions: Combine butter and sugar and cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, to hard crack. Pour into a buttered cookie sheet.  Cool slightly, then top with chocolate chips.  Let chocolate sit until melted and then gently spread over the top.
author: Jennifer Malan
